video_id: Kenichi Ebina Performs an Epic Matrix- Style Martial Arts Dance - America's Got Talent.mp4.mp3
transcript: He does a little dance routine. Oh, dance routine? Yup. So we're gonna battle. Is that what you do? Yeah, dance-ish performance. What's up, man? Hi. How you doing? Very good. Hi, what's your name? My name is Kenichi. I'm 38 years old. I'm from Tokyo, Japan. And I'll be performing a dance-ish performance. A little bit of robots and dance and martial arts and mime. Wow. Dancing is my passion. My dream is to do my full-length one-month show in Vegas. That's why I'm here. I'm ready to see something I've never seen seen before You go give it to her. Yeah, go get her man. Have fun out there Look at you. Oh, what's your name? My name is Kenny Chi Evina. What is your act? My act is dance-ish performance. Dance-ish? Ish, yes. Okay. How did you get into dance-ish kind of performing? I knew only the one step called the running man. And when I did it, people got loud. And I was like, yeah. And I was like, oh, I feel good. All right. Good luck. Have fun. Thank you. Thank you. Oh, my God. Oh, my God. Wow. Oh, my God. Being the youngest judge on the panel, I can say that was off the chain. Thank you. Honey, you're no spring chicken. What are you talking about? Oh, I am. I thought your head had come off your body. It was that convincing. We see a lot of guys who come on stage who try to do what you do. They can't do it, and you have a story to tell. You know, you were doing The Matrix there. That was the whole movie all in 90 seconds it was magnificent i could watch you all day i'm telling you that is the best dance we have seen in chicago it's the best dance i've seen in a long time thank you you told a great story you were wonderful thank you
